text: # From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: [Analysis](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/analyses/index.html)

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: A well-functioning IT environment is essential for wholesale and retail, but with increasing digitalization, vulnerability to cyber threats also increases. The consequences of a cyber attack can be far-reaching, although entrepreneurs in the sector underestimate the dangers. It is no longer a question of whether a retail entrepreneur will be hacked, but when.

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: The digitalization of the Dutch economy has taken off enormously in recent years and will only increase in the future, and the wholesale and retail sector is no exception. From automated inventory systems to online sales channels, digital transactions and customer data; a well-functioning IT environment is essential. However, as the IT environment grows, so does the vulnerability to cyber threats.

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: The annual cybersecurity report from ABN AMRO and MWM2 from May 2025 shows that cybercrime now represents a strategic risk for companies. Yet the 788 Dutch organizations surveyed appear to underestimate the threat; Small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and self-employed people in particular have excessive confidence in their cyber resilience, while their precautionary measures are often limited. This is at odds with the reality in which such cyber attacks are becoming increasingly sophisticated and the need for prevention, detection and recovery measures is growing.

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: [Read also: Cyber resilience of entrepreneurs lags behind reality](/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/alle-sectoren/cyberweerbaarheid-ondernemers-blijft-achter-bij-reueel.html)

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: This analysis builds on the research of ABN AMRO and MWM2, with a specific focus on the survey results for wholesale and retail. This sector works with large amounts of customer and transaction data and is highly dependent on logistics and digital chains. Moreover, the wholesale and retail sector consists of a wide range of companies - from independent retailers to multinationals with their own IT infrastructure - so the resilience and risks can vary widely. In the survey, 81 respondents indicated that they worked in this sector. This analysis maps the most important insights, risks, points of interest and examples based on the survey results.

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: ## Perception, incidents and vulnerabilities in the retail sector

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: Half of the retail organizations in the survey believe that cybercrime poses 'some risk' to business operations. More than 80 percent of all organizations indicate that they have experienced cybercrime in the past and more than 60 percent indicate that this has occurred in the past twelve months. While almost all organizations in the sector have had to deal with a cyber incident in the past, only 37 percent indicate that they can estimate the costs to their organization of shutting down business operations after a serious cyber attack.

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: The costs of such an attack are obvious; IT security company ESET calculated a few years ago that the damage to SMEs per incident is around [270,000](https://www.overzicht.nl/cybersecurity/nieuws/veiligsincident-kost-een-onderneming--270000.html) euros. This raises the question of whether the risk is not being underestimated. Most of the damage suffered by wholesale and retail organizations last year was the result of operational disruptions, followed by financial damage (such as ransom payments, recovery costs or fines) and data loss.

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: In addition, damage to image must be taken into account. Retail is visible, recognizable and in direct contact with the consumer, which means that cyber incidents quickly enter the public sphere and can spread rapidly. Organizations in the sector process large amounts of personal data such as names and addresses, as well as financially sensitive customer data every day. Customers feel personally affected when this data is stolen. In a highly competitive sector, a breakdown in trust can make the difference in customer choice.

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: [Read also: Companies take the lead for a healthy society](/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/healthcare/bedrijven-aan-zet-voor-een-gezonde-samenleving.html)

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text:
- [Marks & Spencer](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c93llkg4n51o), a leading British department store, was hit by a ransomware attack in April 2025. A hacker group infiltrated the organization's online infrastructure, most likely due to human error on the part of an employee. No online orders could be placed for almost a month and many operational disruptions occurred, which are likely to continue until July. The total damage is estimated at 300 million euros.
- Floor covering and window decoration company [Carpetright](https://www.nu.nl/tweakers/6303524/carpetright-waarschouwt-clients-voor-datalek.html) discovered a data breach in February 2024. The company stated that it was not certain whether data had actually been stolen, but the personal and password data of 30,000 Dutch and Belgian customers may have been stolen. If this is the case, it increases the risk of phishing attempts among customers, with all the associated reputational risks for Carpetright.
- [Blue Yonder](https://www.warehousetotaal.nl/nieuws/jumbo-en-hema-voelen-computerhack-blue-yonder/135641/), supplier of supply chain management software to Jumbo and Hema, among others, was hit by a ransomware attack in November 2024. The logistics chain of both Jumbo and Hema was disrupted as a result, but customers did not experience any inconvenience due to backup systems.
- The Dutch jeans maker and seller Denham [DENHAM](https://thecyberexpress.com/denham-the-jeanmaker-data-breach/) was hit by a cyber attack by the ransomware group Akira in 2024. HR data, personal documents, customer data and parts of the financial administration were stolen. A total of 100 gigabites of sensitive data was stolen and a ransom was demanded.
- The French luxury brand [Dior](https://retailtrends.nl/news/75863/ook-dior-slachtoffer-van-cyberattack-klantgegevens-op-straat) established in May 2025 that a data breach had occurred in which personal data of a group of its most wealthy customers had been stolen. Customers' financial information was spared, but stolen data such as email addresses, telephone numbers and purchase details make these customers more vulnerable to abuse by cyber criminals.

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: Figure 1 shows the different forms of cybercrime that wholesale and retail organizations have faced in the past. It is clear that phishing is the most common threat. In general, there are no major differences in threats between entrepreneurs from all sectors surveyed and those from wholesale and retail, except that retailers appear to have relatively little experience with data breaches.

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: ## Organizations optimistic, but there are concerns

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: Organizations are generally optimistic about their own cyber resilience. More than a third indicate that it is 'not difficult at all' for their organization to get and maintain cybersecurity in good order, and another 47 percent state that this is 'a little difficult'. Almost all respondents indicate that they will spend at least as much on cybersecurity in 2025 as in the previous year. A third indicate that they will spend more. It is striking that it appears that the majority of the measures are aimed at preventing an attack, for example by using a firewall, antivirus programs and timely software updates.

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: Significantly less is invested in recovery, response and detection measures. Prevention is important, but it is also important to take measures into account after an attack is discovered. It now seems that it is no longer a question of whether an entrepreneur will be hacked, but when. Organizations would do well to have a tested investigation procedure and a proven response and recovery plan ready.

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: The biggest challenges within the sector mainly lie in the speed at which new threats emerge (see Figure 2). Improving the discipline of one's own employees and the costs associated with getting one's own cybersecurity in order are also mentioned. Developments in the field of artificial intelligence (AI) are also following each other at lightning speed. Nearly half of organizations see innovations in AI as a threat to cybersecurity. For example, where phishing previously required manual work, attackers can now automate the entire process using 'Agentic AI' - systems that can autonomously plan and carry out actions. At the same time, 53 percent see AI as an opportunity to strengthen cybersecurity.

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: NIS2 – the successor to the Network and Information Security Directive (NIS) – came into force in the European Union in October 2024. To comply with the directive, the Netherlands is introducing national legislation in the form of the Cybersecurity Act (Cbw/NIS2) and the Resilience of Critical Entities Act (Wwke/CER), which are expected to come into force in the third quarter of 2025. These laws apply to organizations operating in defined 'critical, essential and important' sectors. Organizations that fall under the legislation must comply with registration, care and reporting obligations and will be subject to supervision.

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: [Read also: Cybersecurity Act: prepare yourself for stricter rules](/nl/zakelijk/insights/cybersecurity/veiligheid/cybersecuritywet-prepare-je-voor-op-stricter-rules.html)

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: A wholesale and retail organization falls under the new legislation if this organization focuses on the processing or distribution of foodstuffs ([Appendix II](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/NL/TXT/HTML/?uri=CELEX:32022L2555&qid=1695042128616#anx_II)) and if the organization has more than fifty employees and a turnover of 10 million euros. Organizations that fall outside these criteria are not automatically 'in scope', but can still be designated by the government because of their crucial social role. If companies do not have to comply with the NIS2 legislation, the consequences of the law may still be felt, for example when doing business within the chain with an organization that is subject to the legislation. In order to meet their own obligations, organizations that are NIS2-mandated can require their chain partners to be NIS2-compliant.

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: Six in ten wholesale and retail organizations indicate that they are not aware of the NIS2 obligations. To know whether an organization falls under the NIS2 directive and what this means, our report '[Cyber ​​resilience of entrepreneurs lags behind reality](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/alle-sectoren/cyberweerbaarheid-ondernemers-blijft-achter-bij-reueel.html)' provides insight, as well as completing the Central Government's Self-Evaluation.

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: ## Concrete tips for wholesale and retail organizations

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text:
- Increasing cyber security awareness among employees, for example by offering internal or external training or with phishing simulations.
- Make structural agreements with suppliers or other partners in the supply chain about cyber risks and security.
- Implementing measures not only aimed at prevention, but also in the areas of recovery, response and detection, for example with regular offline backups, testing recovery processes, developing a script and an internal and external communication plan.
- Structural budgeting for cybersecurity, regardless of the size of the organization.
- Providing insight into NIS2 obligations, not only for your own organization but also for chain partners.
- Watch our webinar on cybersecurity in the retail sector: [Protect your chain and reputation](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/cybersecurity/veilig/protect-je-keten-en-reputatie.html)

metadata.source_title: From warehouse to online store: cybercrime touches the heart of retail

text: Today's store is more than just a pantry. It offers customers advice and inspiration and is the place to be in contact with customers. Due to the Ukraine crisis and significant inflation, consumer confidence has fallen sharply and this affects consumer behavior. Retailers are also faced with significant cost increases in the areas of purchasing, rent, personnel costs, Covid recovery payments and energy. Circumstances remain challenging for many retailers even after Covid.
